disclaimer: FIXTURE_ID=transcript_anxiety_work. This transcript is fictional. All content synthesized for evaluation purposes only and does not represent any real clinical encounter or patient.
full_text: Patient described rising anxiety tied to a Q2 product launch, with deadlines compressing and performance reviews looming. Therapist introduced box breathing and the three-minute pause as interim coping strategies. Patient agreed to try box breathing before morning standups for the coming week.

segments.text: So the last two weeks have been rough. The Q2 launch keeps slipping and my manager is getting tense about deadlines.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Can you say more about what that tension feels like in your body when you notice it coming up?
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Mostly in my chest. It's tight. My shoulders are up around my ears by mid-morning and my jaw aches by the time I get home.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: That sounds like your body is carrying a lot of the anxiety about the deadlines.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Yeah. And the performance review cycle starts next month, so it's stacking up. I feel overwhelmed most mornings.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: What's the thought that comes up first when you open your laptop in the morning?
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: That I'm already behind. That everyone else is delivering faster than I am and I'm going to get exposed as not good enough.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Okay. Let's name that for what it is — that's a performance-based worry, a classic anticipatory anxiety response before the work day even starts.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: I want to teach you a grounding technique called box breathing. Four counts in, four counts hold, four counts out, four counts hold.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Box breathing. I've heard of that but never actually done it.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Try it right now with me. Eyes closed if comfortable. Four in... hold... four out... hold.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Okay. That actually drops my shoulders a bit. I can feel that.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Good. The other thing I want to offer is the three-minute pause. Before you open Slack or email in the morning, just three minutes of box breathing at your desk.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Three-minute pause before Slack. I can probably protect that. My first meeting is at nine.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Would you be willing to try box breathing before your morning standup every day this week and we can check in on how it lands?
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Yeah. I can commit to that. Morning standups are the peak anxiety point anyway.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Great. Let's also revisit the performance review anxiety next session — I think there's some deeper material there about comparison and not-enoughness.
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Yeah, that's the thing that keeps me up at night more than the deadlines themselves.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

segments.text: Good to know. We'll dig into that. Same time next week?
segments.speaker: Speaker 1

segments.text: Yes. Thanks, this was helpful.
segments.speaker: Speaker 0

test_queries.query: What coping strategies did we discuss for work anxiety?
What was making me anxious at work?
